concepts.title: Consumer Protection Act, 1986
concepts.description: The Consumer Protection Act, 1986 is a significant legislation that provides legal protection to consumers in India. It defines consumer as a person who buys goods or hires services for consideration.

concepts.title: Rights of Consumers
concepts.description: The Consumer Protection Act, 1986 grants three fundamental rights to consumers: Right to Safety, Right to be Informed, and Right to Seek Redressal.

concepts.title: Types of Consumer Disputes
concepts.description: Consumer disputes can be classified into three categories: disputes related to defects in goods or services, disputes related to deficiency in goods or services, and disputes related to impositional charges.

concepts.title: Consumer Protection Act, 1986, Key Sections
concepts.description: To remember the key sections of the Consumer Protection Act, 1986, use the mnemonic 'DICE': Section 2 (definition of consumer), Section 3 (definition of defects), Section 11 (Consumer Dispute Redressal Agencies), and Section 18 (Composition of the Central Consumer Protection Authority).

concepts.title: Consumer Dispute Redressal Agencies
concepts.description: Students often confuse the three-tier structure of Consumer Dispute Redressal Agencies: District Commission, State Commission, and National Commission. To avoid this mistake, remember that the District Commission handles disputes up to Rs. 1 crore, the State Commission handles disputes above Rs. 1 crore but below Rs. 10 crores, and the National Commission handles disputes above Rs. 10 crores.

concepts.title: Consumer Protection Act, 1986, Objectives
concepts.description: The Consumer Protection Act, 1986 has five key objectives: to provide better protection to consumers, to promote and protect consumer rights, to establish consumer councils and other authorities for the promotion and protection of consumer interests, to make provision for the timely and effective disposal of consumer disputes, and to generate awareness among consumers.

concepts.title: Consumer Education and Research Centre (CERC)
concepts.description: Consumer Education and Research Centre (CERC) is a non-governmental organization that works towards promoting consumer awareness and education in India. It was established in 1973 and is based in Mumbai.

concepts.title: Role of Consumer Protection Act, 1986, in Promoting Consumer Welfare
concepts.description: The Consumer Protection Act, 1986 plays a crucial role in promoting consumer welfare by providing a legal framework for consumer protection, establishing a three-tier structure for the redressal of consumer disputes, and generating awareness among consumers about their rights and responsibilities.
